query: our PM just dropped a doc with 7 acceptance criteria for the new 'invite-a-friend' flow, can you turn them into a .feature file we can actually run? she specifically asked for it in given/when/then so she can review it before we wire up the steps

I inherited this feature file from an offshore vendor and honestly it reads like a recorded Selenium script - every step is 'click this selector, type that into #email'. our product owner refuses to look at it. can you refactor it into something declarative

setting up vitest-cucumber for the first time in our nx monorepo - the cart logic lives in libs/checkout/src and I need .feature files alongside the cart.ts. any idea how to wire the vitest config so it picks up *.steps.ts but doesn't try to execute the .feature files as tests?

we're moving from @cucumber/cucumber to playwright-bdd. how do I share auth between scenarios using fixtures? right now in cucumber-js I have a World class with the page reference but I think fixtures supersede that pattern

is this a good case for a scenario outline or should I just write separate scenarios? the dev wants to test 12 different combinations of role × resource × expected-permission. it feels like overkill for one Examples table but I'm not sure

our team is doing example mapping next sprint for the refund-eligibility feature, what's the canonical structure for the yellow/blue/green/red cards and how do those translate into Rule blocks in the .feature file we'd write afterwards

what's the Rule keyword for in Gherkin 6? we have a bunch of older .feature files without it and a colleague is insisting we should add Rule blocks everywhere. is that actually better or is it noise if a feature only has one rule

I have a scenario where the 'When' step needs to await a request, store its response, and then a later 'Then' compares against it. how do I share state between steps in @amiceli/vitest-cucumber - I've been using a module-level let but it's leaking between scenarios

our step definitions are exploding - every feature file has its own *.steps.ts and we have a lot of duplication. should I organize them by domain (auth.steps.ts, cart.steps.ts) instead? what does the cucumber community actually recommend in 2026

we're picking between cucumber.js + playwright-as-library vs playwright-bdd by vitalets. our team is mostly typescript, we don't need cross-language gherkin reuse, and we already use Playwright's html reporter heavily. which way
write a vitest test that checks the price calculator returns 42 when given an empty cart and a 10% off coupon. no need for bdd framing, just a simple it() with expect()
I have a flaky playwright test() that fails intermittently when running 4 workers in parallel - the login form sometimes loads slow. how do I add a retry only for that specific test
best way to test a React useReducer hook with @testing-library/react? I want to fire actions and assert on the state - looking at the renderHook + act approach but the typings are weird

our product manager asked me to write the acceptance criteria for the new password-reset flow. she just wants the criteria themselves in a google doc, not tests. how should I structure that - given/when/then or just bullet points of requirements

I'm doing TDD on a new pricing service - write a failing test first, then the simplest code to pass, refactor. need help thinking about what the first failing test should be for 'calculate volume discount'. red-green-refactor not bdd
we're seeing checkout p99 latency creep up to 800ms in prod. need to set up a k6 load test that ramps to 500 concurrent users and asserts p99 stays under 500ms. any thoughts on the script structure
is istanbul still the best choice for code coverage on a vitest setup or should I be using v8 coverage now? we just want a coverage badge in the readme and a CI gate at 80% lines
writing a pact contract test for the order-service consumer of the inventory-service. how do I structure the consumer-driven contract so the inventory team can verify against it in their CI
how should I name unit tests - I've seen 'should_do_X_when_Y', 'it does X when Y', 'it_X_Y'. what's the modern js/ts convention with vitest and is there a lint rule that enforces it

I want to convert the QA team's manual regression checklist (around 80 steps in a google sheet) into something that runs in CI. they currently click through it once per release. before I do anything tool-specific - what's the right shape for this, automated UI tests, API tests, or behavior-driven scenarios
